_publications._publicationTitle: Unbalancing cAMP and Ras/MAPK pathways as a therapeutic strategy for cutaneous neurofibromas.

_contextSnippet: ogerin treatment was elevating cAMP levels in cNF SCs. A similar viability pattern was observed in ogerin-treated SC cultures, alone or in cocultures, with no effect on FBs viability
details: At 15 μM reduced SC viability and slightly decreased SC proliferation in single cultures and SC-FB cocultures with no effect on FBs. Elevated cAMP levels in cNF SCs. In combination with selumetinib enhanced loss of viability, increased apoptosis, and induced promyelinization compared to selumetinib alone
